content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

No source changes were made. I benchmarked the requested debug vs release paths first; the release build did not materially improve the hot CLI lifecycle test and made the conformance/full CLI timings slightly worse overall, so I intentionally did not add `[profile.test] opt-level` tuning to `Cargo.toml`.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Measure the target single CLI lifecycle test in debug and release profiles to validate whether optimization meaningfully reduces runtime.
2. [Skipped] Apply a `Cargo.toml` test-profile optimization only if the benchmark data shows a significant win.
3. [Completed] Run project verification commands on the final tree and record the benchmark/validation results.

## Validation Evidence

- Single lifecycle CLI test, debug: `cargo test --locked --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error -- --exact` passed in 69.65s test time (`real 69.73`).
- Single lifecycle CLI test, release: `cargo test --locked --release --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error -- --exact` passed in 65.63s test time (`real 103.17` including release compilation).
- Full CLI suite, debug: `cargo test --locked --features test-stub --test cli` passed with 268 passed / 0 failed / 2 ignored in 332.03s (`real 332.15`).
- Full CLI suite, release: `cargo test --locked --release --features test-stub --test cli` passed with 268 passed / 0 failed / 2 ignored in 339.60s (`real 339.72`).
- Isolated conformance, debug: `cargo test --locked --features test-stub --test cli conformance_full_suite_passes -- --exact` passed in 322.12s (`real 322.23`).
- Isolated conformance, release: `cargo test --locked --release --features test-stub --test cli conformance_full_suite_passes -- --exact` passed in 344.41s (`real 344.51`).
- Final verification on the unchanged tree passed: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo clippy --locked --features test-stub -- -D warnings`; `cargo test --locked --features test-stub` (`real 443.57`); `nix build`.

## Outstanding Risks

- The underlying performance problem remains unresolved: `cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error` is still far above the 15 second target in both profiles (69.65s debug test time vs 65.63s release test time).
- The release-mode hypothesis was not validated for conformance or the full CLI suite: isolated conformance was slower in release (344.41s) than debug (322.12s), and the full CLI suite was also slower in release (339.60s) than debug (332.03s).
- I did not benchmark intermediate `profile.test` settings such as `opt-level = 1` because the full release baseline already failed to show a compelling runtime win; adding compile cost without evidence would be speculative.
